Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The user wants an analysis of a provided event log for a Customer Support Ticket Resolution process I need to identify slow cases determine root causes of delays focusing on escalations waiting times etc and provide insights recommendations to fix these bottlenecks

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Data Event Log Case 101 Receive 03 01 08 00 Triage 08 10 plus 10m Assign 08 20 plus 10m Investigate 09 00 plus 40m Resolve 10 00 plus 60m Close 10 15 plus 15m Total Time 08 00 to 10 15 equals 2 hours 15 minutes No escalation Case 102 Receive 03 01 08 05 Triage 08 30 plus 25m Assign 09 00 plus 30m Escalate 11 30 plus 150m from Assign plus 90m from Investigate which is missing here Wait there is no initial Investigate it goes straight to Escalate after Assign Investigate 14 00 plus 150m from Escalate Resolve 03 02 09 00 plus 19h from Investigate Overnight delay Close 03 02 09 15 plus 15m Total Time 08 05 to 03 02 09 15 equals 25 hours 10 minutes Has escalation Case 103 Receive 03 01 08 10 Triage 08 15 plus 5m Assign 08 30 plus 15m Investigate 08 45 plus 15m Resolve 09 15 plus 30m Close 09 30 plus 15m Total Time 08 10 to 09 30 equals 1 hour 20 minutes No escalation Case 104 Receive 03 01 08 20 Triage 09 00 plus 40m Assign 09 30 plus 30m Investigate 13 00 plus 210m equals 3 point 5h Resolve 03 02 08 00 plus 19h Overnight delay Close 03 02 08 30 plus 30m Total Time 08 20 to 03 02 08 30 equals 24 hours 10 minutes No escalation but massive delay between Investigate and Resolve Case 105 Receive 03 01 08 25 Triage 08 35 plus 10m Assign 09 00 plus 25m Investigate 09 10 plus 10m Escalate 10 00 plus 50m from Investigate Investigate L2 03 02 14 00 plus 28h Massive delay after escalation Resolve 03 03 09 00 plus 19h Another overnight delay Close 03 03 09 30 plus 30m Total Time 08 25 to 03 03 09 30 equals 49 hours 5 minutes Has escalation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Wait there is no initial Investigate it goes straight to Escalate after Assign

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Calculate and Compare Total Resolution Times Case 103 1h 20m Fastest Case 101 2h 15m Case 104 24h 10m Case 102 25h 10m Case 105 49h 5m Slowest Significantly longer cases 102 104 and 105 They take roughly 10x to 40x longer than the baseline 101 and 103

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Identify Root Causes of Performance Issues Escalation Bottleneck Cases 102 and 105 Whenever a ticket is escalated to a Level 2 Agent there is a massive delay before the Level 2 agent even begins investigating Case 102 Escalated at 11 30 Level 2 Investigation starts at 14 00 2 point 5h wait Case 105 Escalated at 10 00 Level 2 Investigation starts at 14 00 the next day 28h wait This points to a severe lack of capacity or poor handoff protocols for Level 2 agents

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Overnight Delays Queue Stagnation Cases 102 104 105 There are suspicious 19 hour delays between Investigate and Resolve Case 104 or Investigate and Resolve after L2 Case 102 and 105 Case 102 Investigated at 14 00 Resolved next day at 09 00 Case 104 Investigated at 13 00 Resolved next day at 08 00 Case 105 Investigated at 14 00 Resolved two days later at 09 00

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This strongly suggests end of day batching lack of after hours support or tickets sitting in a queue waiting for a specific shift to handle resolutions

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Pre Investigation Delays Case 104 Case 104 took 3 point 5 hours just to move from Assign to Level 1 Agent to Investigate Issue whereas baseline cases took 15 to 40 minutes

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Formulate Insights and Recommendations Escalation Process The handoff to Level 2 is highly inefficient Recommendation Implement a direct routing or alerting system for Level 2 agents Maybe assign Level 2 agents to specific queues or provide better SLA tracking for escalations Shift Handoffs After Hours Policy The recurring 19 hour gap indicates work is being pushed to the next day Recommendation Either implement 24 by 7 support for active tickets or establish a clear end of day protocol where tickets are handed off to an evening shift rather than sitting idle Level 1 Agent Workload Availability Case 104 shows a Level 1 agent taking 3 point 5 hours to start investigating Recommendation Investigate Level 1 agent workloads or implement automated reminders for assigned tickets Process Standardization Baseline cases 101 and 103 prove the process can run smoothly in 1 to 2 hours The delays are entirely systemic waiting not due to the complexity of the issue itself investigation and resolve times when they happen are relatively short

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Structure the Output Part 1 Identification of Delayed Cases Present the math clearly separating standard vs delayed cases Part 2 Root Causes of Performance Issues Group by theme Escalation Handoffs Overnight Stagnation Pre investigation lag Part 3 Insights and Recommendations Actionable steps mapped to the root causes

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Refine the Output Ensure professional tone clear formatting bullet points bold text and directly answer all parts of the prompt Double check timestamps to ensure accuracy Self Correction during drafting Make sure to note that Case 104 did not have an escalation but still suffered the overnight delay This proves the overnight delay is a systemic issue independent of the escalation process Case 105 had both an escalation delay and an overnight delay making it the worst offender

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Final Polish Proceed to generate response
